zh.lifeSummary: 當Lesceline de Clinton於1124年出生在法國諾門迪曼其時，她的父親Sir Geoffrey de Clinton22歲，母親Lasceline22歲。她和Sir Norman de Verdun of Farnham Royal and  Alton至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1207年9月29日之後。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lesceline de Clinton was born in 1124, in Manche, Normandy, France, her father, Sir Geoffrey de Clinton, was 22 and her mother, Lasceline, was 22. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Norman de Verdun of Farnham Royal and  Alton. She died after 29 September 1207.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lesceline de Clinton est née en 1124, à Manche, Normandie, France, son père, Sir Geoffrey de Clinton, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Lasceline, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Norman de Verdun of Farnham Royal and  Alton. Elle est décédée après 29 septembre 1207.

ja.lifeSummary: Lesceline de Clintonが1124年にフランスノーマンディマンチェで生まれた時，父Sir Geoffrey de Clintonは22歳，母Lascelineは22歳であった。彼女はSir Norman de Verdun of Farnham Royal and  Altonとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1207年9月29日以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lesceline de Clinton(이)가 1124년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey de Clinton(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lasceline(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Sir Norman de Verdun of Farnham Royal and  Alton(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1207년 9월 29일 이후 사망했다.
